title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. subviriduliceps
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. subviriduliceps, also known as the green hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is a type of fern-leaved hawk's beard, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and fern-like leaves. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Hieracium lachenalii subsp. subviriduliceps is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting liver function.
